meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Learn how to implement a professional SaaS starter kit to solve version control issues using Antigravity and Claude Code as a freelancer.
content.answer_block: The lack of version control is a ticking time bomb for freelancers. By integrating a structured SaaS starter kit, you gain immediate access to automated Git workflows, environment segregation, and rollback capabilities. This architecture transforms your messy coding sessions into a professional, scalable pipeline that clients will trust and pay for.
content.intro:
You’re three hours away from a deadline, the client wants a 'quick tweak,' and suddenly, your local environment is a graveyard of broken features and forgotten logic. Without version control, you aren't building software; you're playing a high-stakes game of Jenga where one wrong move collapses your entire reputation.

But the professional path isn't about spending weeks setting up infrastructure. It’s about leveraging a SaaS starter kit that enforces discipline from the first commit. By combining the rapid scaffolding of Antigravity with the autonomous precision of Claude Code, you can turn a chaotic workflow into a bulletproof, version-controlled engine that scales with your business.

steps.title: Initialize the Starter Kit
steps.description: Establish a robust foundation that includes pre-configured Git hooks and environment management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a production-ready SaaS starter kit with Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase, ensuring all folders are pre-initialized with a Git repository and a .gitignore tailored for SaaS.

steps.title: Configure Branching Strategy
steps.description: Define a git-flow that allows you to test features in isolation before merging into main.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a concise Git branching strategy for a freelance workflow, focusing on feature-branching for client changes without touching the stable production code.

steps.title: Automate Versioning with AI
steps.description: Use Claude Code to automate commit messages and version tagging, removing the cognitive load of manual tracking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that hooks into pre-commit to summarize staged changes and suggest a semantic versioning tag for the current release.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit "auto-generate conventional commit message for current changes"

steps.title: Implement Environment Segregation
steps.description: Ensure development, staging, and production environments are isolated to prevent accidental data overwrites.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create environment variable templates and a deployment script that switches between dev and prod configurations based on the current branch.

steps.title: Establish Rollback Procedures
steps.description: Create a 'panic button' script that allows you to revert the entire application state in seconds.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a shell script that performs a 'git reset --hard HEAD~1' and cleans the node_modules to ensure a fresh state after a broken deployment.

pro_tips: Always commit your environment setup files before installing dependencies to track dependency drift.
Use 'git stash' before switching branches to preserve client-specific tweaks that aren't ready for production.
Tag every client delivery with a semantic version number to manage expectations and billing milestones.
Integrate a CI/CD pipeline early; if it isn't deployed to a staging URL, it doesn't exist for the client.
Keep your .env files out of version control using a template system to avoid leaking production secrets.
resources: Git Best Practices for Freelancers: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, start building your first feature module and practice the 'commit-test-deploy' cycle to build muscle memory.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours on manual setup and start shipping like a pro. Your clients deserve reliable, version-controlled code, and you deserve a workflow that doesn't cause burnout. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
